title: Glacier Sensor Net: Well-Spread Seismic Beacon Layout
strategies: incircle baseline: N beacons equally spaced on the survey triangle's incircle (reproduces the checker baseline, min area dominated by 3-consecutive-vertex slivers)
multi-restart uniform random sampling inside the triangle, keeping the layout with the largest minimum triangle area

bottleneck-driven local search: repeatedly find the smallest-area triple and push one vertex perpendicular to its opposite edge, accepting moves that raise the global minimum area, over several seeded restarts
structured Heilbronn-style constructions (boundary + interior lattices, perturbed grids) tuned to avoid thin triangles across the whole region
